title: Testing Robot

content.problemStatement:
Chef has bought a new robot, which will be used for delivering dishes to his customers. He started testing the robot by letting it move on a line.

Initially, the robot is placed at the coordinate x=Xx = Xx=X. Then, it should execute a sequence of NNN commands, described by a string SSS with length NNN. Each character of this string is either 'L' or 'R', denoting that the robot should walk one step to the left (decreasing xxx by 111) or to the right (increasing xxx by 111), respectively.

How many distinct points are visited by the robot when it has executed all the commands? A point ppp is visited by the robot if ppp is an integer and the robot's position before or after executing some command is x=px = px=p.
constraints: S consists only of characters 'L' and 'R'
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains two integers N and X — the length of the command string and the initial coordinate of the robot. The second line contains a string S of length N consisting only of characters 'L' and 'R'.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing one integer — the number of distinct integer points visited by the robot (including the starting point).

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: positions visited are 10,11,12,11,10,9,8 → distinct {8,9,10,11,12} = 5. Test case 2: positions visited are 0,-1,-2 → distinct {0,-1,-2} = 3.

hidden.label: stress case 3
hidden.input:
10
1 0
L
1 0
R
1 1
L
1 -1
R
2 0
LR
2 0
RL
3 5
RRL
3 -5
LLR
4 0
RRLL
5 2
LRLRL

solution.approach: The robot moves one step left or right according to each character of the command string. While simulating the moves we keep a set of all positions that have been visited (including the starting position). After processing the whole string the size of the set is the answer. The simulation is linear in the length of the string and uses O(N) additional memory for the set.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) for the whole input.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) per test case for the hash set storing visited positions.

tcGeneratorPython:
def generate_case(i, RINT, POWER):
    # This function prints the input for hidden testcase i (1-indexed).
    # For simplicity we use deterministic patterns; random helpers are available.
    if i == 1:
        print(1)
        print('1 0')
        print('L')
    elif i == 2:
        print(1)
        print('1 0')
        print('R')
    elif i == 3:
        print(1)
        print('1 5')
        print('L')
    elif i == 4:
        print(1)
        print('1 -5')
        print('R')
    elif i == 5:
        print(1)
        print('15 1000000')
        print('R'*15)
    elif i == 6:
        print(1)
        print('15 -1000000')
        print('L'*15)
    elif i == 7:
        print(1)
        print('15 0')
        print('RLRLRLRLRLRLRLR')
    elif i == 8:
        print(1)
        print('15 0')
        print('R'*15)
    elif i == 9:
        print(1)
        print('10 0')
        print('L'*10)
    elif i == 10:
        print(1)
        print('10 0')
        print('R'*10)
    elif i == 11:
        print(1)
        print('10 0')
        print('RRRRLLLLRR')
    elif i == 12:
        print(1)
        print('10 0')
        print('LLLLRRRRLL')
    elif i == 13:
        print(1)
        print('15 999999')
        print('RRRLLRLRRLLRLRL')
    elif i == 14:
        print(1)
        print('15 -999999')
        print('LLLRRLLRRLLLRRL')
    elif i == 15:
        print(1)
        print('13 7')
        print('RLRRLLRLRLLRR')
    elif i == 16:
        print(1)
        print('2 0')
        print('LR')
    elif i == 17:
        print(3)
        print('3 0')
        print('RRL')
        print('4 5')
        print('LLRR')
        print('2 -2')
        print('RL')
    elif i == 18:
        print(5)
        print('1 0')
        print('L')
        print('1 0')
        print('R')
        print('2 10')
        print('LR')
        print('3 -5')
        print('RRR')
        print('4 2')
        print('LLRL')
    elif i == 19:
        print(10)
        cases = [
            ('1 0','L'),('1 0','R'),('1 1','L'),('1 -1','R'),
            ('2 0','LR'),('2 0','RL'),('3 5','RRL'),('3 -5','LLR'),
            ('4 0','RRLL'),('5 2','LRLRL')
        ]
        for header, s in cases:
            print(header)
            print(s)
    elif i == 20:
        print(20)
        for v in range(0,10):
            print(f'1 {v}')
            print('L')
            print(f'1 {-v}')
            print('R')
    else:
        # default simple case
        print(1)
        n = RINT(1, 10)
        x = RINT(-100, 100)
        print(f'{n} {x}')
        print(''.join('L' if RINT(0,1)==0 else 'R' for _ in range(n)))
